generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance JetsonEmbeddedSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson hardware platform providing GPU-accelerated computation for ACC control algorithms and sensor data processing with 100ms real-time latency constraints"
]

instance AccControlLogic : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time Adaptive Cruise Control algorithm implemented for closed-loop speed regulation with 100ms response time requirements"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution 360° LiDAR sensor with ±10mm measurement accuracy for obstacle detection and spatial mapping at 30Hz sampling frequency"
    DTDFVocab:producedData RawLiDARPointClouds, LiDARObjectTracks
]

instance RealSenseD435iCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel RealSense depth camera providing 640×480 resolution 3D perception at 30FPS with sub-millimeter depth precision for environmental perception"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CameraDepthData, CameraObjectDetection
]

instance VehicleSpeedController : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Speed controller sensor providing real-time vehicle velocity and acceleration metrics for ACC feedback control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateTelemetry
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance RawLiDARPointClouds : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
    base:desc "Unprocessed 360° LiDAR point cloud data transmitted via UDP/IP at 30Hz for real-time obstacle detection"
]

instance LiDARObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
    base:desc "Structured object tracks derived from LiDAR point cloud clustering and tracking for 2D/3D environmental perception"
]

instance CameraDepthData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom RealSenseD435iCamera
    base:desc "Raw depth images from RealSense camera transmitted via UDP/IP at 30Hz for 3D spatial reconstruction"
]

instance CameraObjectDetection : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom RealSenseD435iCamera
    base:desc "Object detection bounding boxes and segmentation masks derived from camera feeds for environmental awareness"
]

instance VehicleStateTelemetry : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleSpeedController
    base:desc "Continuous vehicle state data including speed, acceleration, and motor actuation status transmitted via ROS2 topics"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance LiDARClusteringAndTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Computational geometry algorithm for segmenting raw LiDAR point clouds into meaningful objects (vehicles, obstacles) with 2D/3D tracking capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LiDARDataTranslationFE
]

instance SensorFusionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Multi-sensor fusion algorithm combining LiDAR and camera data streams for unified environmental perception with sub-100ms processing latency"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PerceptionEnabler
]

instance AccControlAlgorithmModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink-implemented Adaptive Cruise Control algorithm for real-time speed regulation with variable-speed tracking and obstacle avoidance"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlEnabler
]

instance RawLiDARPointCloudsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed LiDAR point cloud data after clustering and tracking, transmitted via ROS2 topics for environmental perception"
    DTDFVocab:fromData RawLiDARPointClouds
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LiDARDataTranslationFE
]

instance CameraDepthDataProcessed : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed depth images from RealSense camera after unit conversion and calibration, transmitted via ROS2 for 3D spatial reconstruction"
    DTDFVocab:fromData CameraDepthData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PerceptionEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateMetrics : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured vehicle state data including speed, acceleration, and motor actuation status after serialization and unit conversion"
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateTelemetry
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlEnabler, MonitoringDashboard
]

// C11: Enablers
instance LiDARDataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional entity for serializing LiDAR data streams, aligning message types, and performing unit conversions (e.g., LiDAR coordinates to metric units) for interoperability"
    DTDFVocab:enables EnvironmentalPerceptionService
]

instance PerceptionEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based node cluster for multi-sensor fusion (LiDAR + camera) providing unified environmental perception with 100ms processing latency"
    DTDFVocab:enables EnvironmentalPerceptionService, ObstacleAvoidanceService
]

instance ControlEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetson-accelerated control processing unit executing ACC algorithms with 100ms closed-loop response time for speed regulation"
    DTDFVocab:enables AdaptiveCruiseControlService
]

instance MonitoringDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Human-in-the-loop visualization interface displaying real-time sensor data, system metrics, and vehicle state for on-site engineers and remote administrators"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeTelemetryService
]

instance ConfigurationManagementEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Centralized SQL database for storing ACC parameters, historical sensor logs, and system configurations with remote access capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:enables RemoteConfigurationService, DataStorageService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeTelemetryService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Continuous streaming of sensor data (LiDAR, camera, speed controller) and system metrics with 100ms latency for real-time monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:provides TelemetryVisualizationInsight, SystemStatusInsight
]

instance AdaptiveCruiseControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Computationally intensive ACC control algorithm execution offloaded from physical vehicle to reduce Jetson load while maintaining 100ms response time"
    DTDFVocab:provides SpeedRegulationAction, ObstacleAvoidanceAction
]

instance RemoteConfigurationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Remote reconfiguration of ACC parameters (cruise speed thresholds, obstacle detection thresholds) via centralized database interface"
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterUpdateAction, ConfigurationValidationInsight
]

instance DataStorageService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Centralized access to historical sensor data and system logs for post-mortem analysis and trend analysis"
    DTDFVocab:provides HistoricalDataInsight, LogAnalysisInsight
]

instance EnvironmentalPerceptionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Unified environmental perception service combining LiDAR and camera data for object detection and tracking in autonomous driving scenarios"
    DTDFVocab:provides PerceptionDataInsight
]

instance ObstacleAvoidanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic obstacle avoidance logic integrated with ACC for real-time path adjustment in variable-speed tracking scenarios"
    DTDFVocab:provides ObstacleAvoidanceAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance TelemetryVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time dashboard visualization of sensor data streams (LiDAR point clouds, camera feeds, speed metrics) for human-in-the-loop monitoring"
]

instance SystemStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Current system health metrics including sensor latency, processing delays, and vehicle state stability indicators"
]

instance SpeedRegulationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic speed adjustment commands generated by ACC algorithm based on perceived traffic conditions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ObstacleAvoidanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time steering and braking commands triggered by obstacle detection to maintain safe following distance"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote modification of ACC parameters (speed thresholds, obstacle detection sensitivity) through centralized database interface"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance PerceptionDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Structured environmental perception data including object tracks, depth maps, and spatial relationships for autonomous driving decision support"
]

instance HistoricalDataInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analysis-ready historical datasets of sensor readings and system events for post-mortem performance evaluation"
]

instance LogAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "System log analysis reports identifying potential issues or performance bottlenecks in ACC operation"
]

instance ConfigurationValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Validation status of remotely configured parameters against system operational constraints and safety limits"
]

// C1: Entities
instance AutonomousVehiclePhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "1/10-scale autonomous vehicle platform with embedded computing (NVIDIA Jetson) for real-time cyber-physical experimentation of ACC systems"
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, RealSenseD435iCamera, VehicleSpeedController, JetsonEmbeddedSystem, AccControlLogic
]

instance AutonomousVehicleDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Digital representation of the 1/10-scale autonomous vehicle enabling real-time simulation, control offloading, and remote management of ACC systems"
    base:contains LiDARDataTranslationFE, PerceptionEnabler, ControlEnabler, MonitoringDashboard, ConfigurationManagementEnabler
    base:isContainedIn AutonomousDrivingEnvironment
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment AutonomousDrivingEnvironment
]

instance HumanInLoopInterface : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Human-in-the-loop interface enabling interactive testing with operators through real-time visualization and control panel"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeTelemetryService
]

// C18: Environments
instance AutonomousDrivingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Operational domain for autonomous driving scenarios including dynamic road conditions, variable-speed tracking, and obstacle avoidance"
    base:contains AutonomousVehiclePhysicalTwin, AutonomousVehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance SimulationEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Controlled testing environment for ACC logic validation using scaled-down physical model and digital twin simulation"
    base:contains AutonomousVehiclePhysicalTwin, AutonomousVehicleDigitalTwin
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** in this DTS is facilitated by the **ACC (Adaptive Cruise Control) Controlling FE**, which computes control commands based on digital twin data (e.g., lead vehicle detection, projected driving path) and translates them into physical actuation. **Command types** include:
  - **Motor Actuation Commands**: Speed adjustments (e.g., throttle/brake signals) derived from speed control algorithms.
  - **Steering Servo Commands**: Directional adjustments based on path planning outputs.
  - **Vehicle State Feedback Loops**: Real-time adjustments to maintain stability (e.g., adaptive cruise control).

**Control message formats** are serialized via the **Data Translation FE**, which ensures compatibility with physical actuators (e.g., converting ROS2 messages to actuator-specific protocols). **Communication protocols** mirror the physical-to-virtual flow:
  - **Intra-entity**: ROS2 for command routing within the Digital Twin Entity.
  - **Inter-entity**: Wi-Fi/UDP for transmitting commands to physical actuators (e.g., motor/steering servos).

**Validation and authentication mechanisms** are not explicitly detailed in the documents, though the *Data Translation FE* likely enforces message integrity checks (e.g., checksums, timestamp validation). **Feedback systems** rely on bidirectional data flows: the Digital Twin Entity monitors **vehicle state** (e.g., speed, steering angle) via sensors and adjusts commands dynamically. **Conflict resolution** is implied to prioritize **safety-critical commands** (e.g., emergency braking) over non-critical adjustments, though explicit prioritization rules are absent. **Command execution confirmation** is inferred through real-time synchronization of digital twin state (e.g., vehicle position) with physical sensors, ensuring consistency.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system enforces a **100 ms clock synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination. This temporal constraint is mandated due to hardware limitations and aligns with safety-critical vehicular application requirements, though stricter standards (e.g., <100 ms) are referenced in related ISO 23247-based architectures. The system’s temporal granularity is dictated by ROS2-based execution, where each Function Entity (FE) is implemented as an individual ROS2 package containing ROS2 nodes. Latency requirements for inter-node communication are implicitly governed by ROS2’s real-time capabilities, though explicit latency benchmarks for services (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking) are not documented. The architecture does not explicitly detail service-specific time scales beyond the 100 ms synchronization baseline, which applies uniformly across the Physical Twin Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity components."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adopts a **ROS2-based microservices architecture**, where each Function Entity (FE) is implemented as an independent ROS2 package containing one or more ROS2 nodes. This design adheres to ISO 23247’s entity-based structure but introduces deviations: the *Physical Twin Entity* aggregates hardware resources (e.g., sensors, actuators) and general automotive functionalities (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*), contrasting with ISO 23247’s OMEs. The *Digital Twin Entity* manages data processing and control mechanisms, while the *User Entity* hosts user-facing components (*3D Visualization* and *Control Panel*). Orchestration is achieved through ROS2’s topic-based and service-based communication, with resource management implicitly handled by the entity’s hardware deployment. System-wide coordination relies on the 100 ms clock synchronization and ROS2’s deterministic execution model, though no explicit orchestration framework (e.g., Kubernetes, EdgeX) is mentioned. The architecture’s scalability is constrained by hardware-specific FE deployments, impacting engineering processes and functional ownership."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard defines Digital Twins as **real-time, high-fidelity computational reflections** of physical manufacturing elements, but it does not provide explicit methodologies for **model validation, verification, uncertainty quantification, or error correction**. The framework implicitly requires **consistency between OMEs and Digital Twin Entities** via observable attributes, though no formalized processes (e.g., comparative analysis, benchmark testing) or standards (e.g., ISO 9001, IEC 62424) are referenced for ensuring fidelity or quality assurance."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Technical Connection** infrastructure is defined by a **multi-layered network architecture**:
  - **Intra-entity communication**: Uses **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)** for software component interaction within the **Digital Twin Entity** and **User Entity**.
  - **Inter-entity communication**: Relies on **Wi-Fi with UDP transport layer** for data exchange between **Physical Twin Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**.
  - **Data Translation FE**: Acts as a central hub for **serialization**, **message type alignment**, and **unit conversions** across all data streams (e.g., LiDAR → object tracks, vehicle state → control signals).
**Bandwidth and latency requirements** are not specified, but real-time **3D visualization** (Fig. 3a) and **ACC control** imply **low-latency (<100ms)** and **high-bandwidth** (≥10Mbps) for sensor data streams. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are not mentioned, but the use of **Wi-Fi/UDP** suggests potential for packet loss mitigation via retransmission (not detailed). **Security protocols** are not explicitly described, though UDP’s stateless nature may require application-layer encryption (not referenced). The system’s **network topology** appears **distributed**, with entities communicating over a shared Wi-Fi network, while ROS2 ensures localized component coordination.

"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin Hosting and Deployment** infrastructure is not explicitly detailed in the provided documents. Key assumptions from the context:
  - **Hosting platforms**: Likely **edge computing** (due to real-time sensor processing and visualization requirements) or **on-premise** (for localized vehicle systems), but **cloud deployment** is not mentioned.
  - **Computational requirements**: High-performance processing for **LiDAR clustering**, **point cloud tracking**, and **3D visualization** (Fig. 3a), suggesting **GPU-accelerated** systems (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson or similar).
  - **Storage requirements**: Implicitly includes **data storage for reports** (via **Reporting FE**) and **point cloud/vehicle state logs**, but no specifics (e.g., capacity, persistence).
  - **Scalability mechanisms**: Not described, but the use of **ROS2** and **modular FE components** (e.g., Data Translation, Controlling FE) suggests **horizontal scalability** via distributed ROS2 nodes.
  - **Deployment models**: Likely **hybrid** (edge for real-time processing, cloud for analytics/reporting), but not confirmed. **Availability/reliability assurance** is not detailed, though real-time operation implies fault-tolerant design (e.g., redundant sensors or processing units, not mentioned).
**Not in Document** for precise details on cloud/edge split, redundancy strategies, or exact hardware specifications.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
    The document explicitly references the **ISO 23247:2021** standard, which defines:
    - **A Digital Twin framework for manufacturing**, structured into four parts (with **Part 2: Reference Architecture** being key).
    - **Entity-based RA** with roles like **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**, **Data Collection/Device Control Entity**, **Digital Twin Entity**, and **User Entity**.
    - **Interoperability standards**: The standard allows flexibility in implementation (e.g., using **OPC UA** for data exchange).
    - **No explicit certification requirements** or compliance frameworks are mentioned, though adherence to ISO 23247 implies alignment with **industry standards for manufacturing DTs**.
    The standard does not detail **versioning** or **evolution governance** for DTs beyond modular design principles.
  "
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents adheres to **safety-critical requirements** as outlined in **ISO 15622:2018** for **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) systems**, emphasizing **performance and functional safety** in intelligent transport systems. While not explicitly referencing **ISO 26262**, the system implicitly aligns with **safety-critical automotive standards** due to its role in **real-time vehicle control**, where **clock synchronization** is critical for ensuring temporal consistency in safety-critical operations.

The documents highlight **safety-critical system considerations** in the context of **interactive Digital Twin cockpits** (as per [37]), where **real-time data synchronization** and **deterministic behavior** are essential for maintaining operational integrity. However, no explicit mention of **cybersecurity protocols** (e.g., RBAC, MFA, HSMs), **authentication systems**, **threat mitigation strategies**, or **ASIL decomposition** is provided. The focus remains on **performance validation** (e.g., ISO 15622) and **functional safety** in **V2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services** (per [36]), where **safety-critical communication** is implied but not detailed in terms of cryptographic or access control mechanisms.

No evidence of **hardware/software security measures**, **fail-safe mechanisms**, or **risk assessment frameworks** (e.g., ISO 26262) is present in the provided documents. The system’s safety considerations are **implicitly tied to automotive standards** (e.g., ISO 15622) rather than explicitly documented in the provided texts."
]
